name: Absolutely Deep Dark Chocolate Fudge Biscuits
recipeIngredient: 4 oz Unsweetened chocolate, broken into 8 pieces
recipeInstructions:
[Note: Instead of the unsweetened chocolate, I just used an extra 4ozs of plain chocolate, and got perfect results. I think as long as you're using really good plain chocolate, say at least 55% cocoa solids, it doesn't really matter!]
1. Preheat the oven to 325°F / 170°C / Gas 3.
2. Sift together the flour, cocoa powder, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.
3. Heat 1 inch of water in the bottom half of a double boiler over medium heat. Place the plain and unsweetened chocolates in the top half of the double boiler. Tightly cover the top with cling film and allow to melt for 12 to 15 minutes. Remove from the heat and stir until smooth. Keep at room temperature until needed.
4. In the bowl of an electric mixer fitted with a paddle, beat together the light brown sugar and butter on medium speed for 1 minute. Scrape down the bowl and beat on high for an additional 30 seconds. Scrape down the bowl.
5. Add the eggs, one at a time, while beating on medium, and stop to scrape down the bowl after incorporating each addition. Add the vanilla extract and beat on medium for 30 seconds. Add the melted chocolate and beat on low for 10 seconds more. Scrape down the bowl and beat for an additional 30 seconds.
6. Gradually add the sifted flour mixture, cocoa powder, baking soda, salt, and chocolate chips to the mixer, beating until thoroughly combined (about 20-30 seconds). Remove the bowl from the mixer and mix the dough thoroughly with a rubber spatula.
7. Portion the batter onto non-stick baking sheets, forming 6 to 8 biscuits per sheet by dropping about 2 level tablespoons of batter for each biscuit. Place the sheets on the top and middle racks of the preheated oven.
8. Bake for 18 to 22 minutes, rotating the sheets from top to bottom halfway through the baking time. Allow the biscuits to cool on the baking sheets for 5 to 6 minutes before transferring them to a cooling rack to thoroughly cool. Store in a sealed plastic container.
Makes 3 to 3.5 dozen biscuits
From Marcel Desaulniers' "Death By Chocolate"
Recipe by: Paul Vincent
